behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all relevant paths (testing/terminal.ts, terminal.tsx, actions.ts, fixtures.ts, AGENTS.md), the terminalAttr constant value 'data-pty-id', the window structure __opencode_e2e.terminal.terminals[id], fields connected/rendered/settled, and helper names waitTerminalReady/runTerminal.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes required behavior and API surface without dictating exact code changes or line numbers.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use flexible discovery (Object.values find, name substring matching for init/connect/render/settle/cleanup) rather than asserting specific function names.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute the module via node --experimental-strip-types, call probe methods, and inspect window state; some integration tests inspect source but core f2p tests run code.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_terminal_probe_tracks_state asserts rendered='hello world' and settled=1 after calls, which a stub returning defaults cannot satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover probe state tracking, cleanup, noop without window, terminal.tsx integration, actions helpers, and fixtures init script setup.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Integration tests) and tagged [static]/[pr_diff].

structured_data_schema.reason: Window state schema {enabled, terminals} and per-terminal fields {connected, rendered, settled} are documented in instruction.md.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers in instruction are consistent with tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files but does not point to specific buggy lines; localization to these files is inherent to the task.
